name: Anise Molasses Brisket Braised in a Wok
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Beef brisket, trimmed of excess fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the beef brisket against the grain into 1/4-inch thick slices.
2. Pierce the eggplant several times with the tines of a fork. Place in a preheated 400°F oven and roast for 3 minutes, or until shriveled and soft. Set aside.
3. Heat the oil in a large wok or large deep skillet. When it begins to smoke, add half of the beef and cook, stirring, until browned. Remove and set aside.
4. Add remaining beef, and cook, stirring, until browned. Remove and set aside.
5. Add onion, ginger, garlic, crushed pepper, star anise, and soy sauce to wok and stir-fry for 10 seconds.
6. Return beef to wok, along with sherry, broth, and molasses. Stir to blend and season with salt and pepper.
7. Cover and simmer over low heat for 50 minutes to 1 hour, until the beef is tender.
8. While beef is cooking, soak fungus in hot water to cover. When swollen and softened, drain and rinse well; set aside.
9. Cut the stem end from the cooked eggplant. Split in half lengthwise and scoop the flesh into a food processor or blender. Puree.
10. Add eggplant puree, drained fungus, and tomatoes to beef mixture and cook for 5 minutes longer.
11. Stir in scallions and drizzle with sesame oil. Serve with rice, if desired.
Serves 4
Per Serving: 445 calories, 38 g protein, 27 g carbohydrate, 19 g fat (5 g saturated), 93 mg cholesterol, 916 mg sodium, 6 g fiber.
